# init(newBigIntFrom:in:) | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ JavaScriptCore ](/documentation/javascriptcore)

- [ JSValue ](/documentation/javascriptcore/jsvalue)

- [ init(newBigIntFrom:in:) ](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-r38z)

- [ JSValue ](/documentation/javascriptcore/jsvalue)

-  init(newBigIntFrom:in:) 

Initializer# init(newBigIntFrom:in:)

iOS 18.0+iPadOS 18.0+Mac Catalyst 18.0+macOS 15.0+tvOS 9.0+visionOS 2.0+

```
init?(
    newBigIntFrom value: Double,
    in context: JSContext
)
```

## [ Parameters ](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-r38z#parameters)

`value`The value of the BigInt JavaScript value being created.

`context`The JSContext to which the resulting JSValue belongs.

## [Return Value](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-r38z#return-value)

The JSValue representing a JavaScript value with type BigInt.

## [Discussion](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-r38z#discussion)

Create a new BigInt value from a double.

If the value is not an integer, an exception is thrown.
